title: SQL Support on ODBC
text: SQL Support on ODBC Both the sql ProcScript instruction and SQL Editor are supported. Allowed Commands For a complete list of SQL commands that can be executed within a user transaction, see your DBMS documentation. The network protocol you use might influence the type of commands that you are allowed to issue. Transaction Control Supported when the DBMS used supports it. The commands that involve transaction management are: commit (ProcScript command) rollback (ProcScript command) commit transaction (depending on the DBMS) rollback transaction (depending on the DBMS) begin transaction (depending on the DBMS) Note: If you perform transaction management, once the transaction finishes, you must ensure that the logon path is returned to the same state as it was before the transaction management was performed. If you do not do this, the ODBC Connector does not understand the correct state of the connection. Monitoring SQL in the Message Frame If requested in the application shell, the SQL generated by Uniface can be sent to the message frame. Error messages from ODBC are always sent to the message frame. The SQL statements in the message frame do not display values for inserts and updates since the parameters are bound by the Connector. To receive all ODBC information messages and the results in the message frame, set the /pri command line switch to 32 or higher. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
